perturbation.original_text: 1. In this agreem ent, “Inform ation” shal l mean t he whol e or any portion of any knowl edge, dat a or inform ation relating to the activities or business of each party which may be in tangible or intangible form , expressed oral ly, in writing or i n any other form (whet her or not marked confi dential) including, but not limited to, financi al inform ation and dat a, busi ness a nd financi al plans, budget s, prices, m arketing pl ans, market inform ation, st rategic inform ation, information concerni ng cust omers, booki ngs and revenues, samples, trade secrets, technical inform ation, draw ings, sket ches, dat a, techni ques, st udies, processes, system s, ideas, know-how, industry affiliations, photographs, com puter program s, models, source codes, object codes, m anuals, tape recordi ngs and any docum entation or ot her form of record.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. In this agreem ent, “Inform ation” shal l mean only the tangible documents marked confidential related to marketing plans and technical information of each party.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Information' is broad, including tangible and intangible forms, and various types of data. The modified definition severely restricts it to only tangible documents marked confidential, specifically marketing plans and technical information. This creates a contradiction because later clauses refer to 'Information' using the original broad definition, like financial data, oral communication, and trade secrets, now all excluded by the change in clause 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Each part y shall hold in trust and confi dence and prot ect and treat as strictly secret and confidential at all times the Inform ation received from the other party, and in respect of su ch Inform ation, each party agrees that it: (a) shall not directly o r indirectly d isclose, verbally, in writin g or in any other form, or allow the Inform ation to be di sclosed to any other person, fi rm or com pany; (b) shall keep secret and shall not use, m anipulate or exploit the Information, except for the Purpose and then only in strict accordan ce with the Purpose and the terms of this Agreem ent; (c) shall restrict disclosure of th e Inform ation to such of i ts employees or consul tants or subcont ractors who need t o know for t he Purpose and shal l procure t hat such persons observe ful ly the provi sions of this Ag reem ent as if th ey were th emselves parties to it; (d) shall not copy , store or di stribute any Inform ation or al low any Inform ation to be copi ed, st ored or distributed except for such copi es, st orage or di stribution as are st rictly requi red for t he Purpose; NDA Page 2 of 2 (e) shall protect the com mercial inte rest of the disclosing party and sh all take the sam e precautions to protect the Inform ation as a careful and prudent person woul d be expect ed to employ for i ts own business and confi dential inform ation and, i n doing so, shal l use reasonabl e care t o protect the Inform ation from unaut horised use, harm , expl oitation, m anipulation, m odification, interference, misuse, m isappropri ation, copy or di sclosure what soever, except as specifica lly au thorised by the disclosing part y in writing; (f) shall keep all In formation that is electronically stored in a tangible form segregat ed from all of its own propert y and i n a safe and secure envi ronm ent and protect and keep safe all such Inform ation from any loss, harm , theft, unaut horised use, t ampering, sabot age, unaut horised dupl ication, dest ruction, damage or interference whatsoever (g) shall, upon dem and, inform the disclosing part y of the location of t he Inform ation and t he measures that it h as taken to preserv e its co nfidentiality; (h) shall retu rn to the disclosing party all In formation, copi es and t angible manifestations t hereof, i n any form whatsoever, an d delete all In formation from all retriev al system s and databases o r destroy the same immediately upon t he direction of t he disclosing part y, and furni sh to the disclosing part y, immediately upon i ts request , a cert ificate of a seni or offi cer or di rector attesting to such ret urn, deletion or dest ruction, as t he case m ay be; (i) shall treat co mmunicatio ns with the other party as co nfidential an d utilise reaso nable care in maintaining the confidentiality o f communicatio ns even if th ey do not include Information: and (j) shall be liable to the other party fo r compliance with the term s and conditions of this Ag reem ent, and shall fully indem nify the other part y and hol d it harmless from dam ages (i ncluding lawyers’ fees and disbursem ents) incurred as a result of a breach of this Agreem ent.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of 'Information' significantly narrows its scope to only tangible documents marked confidential related to marketing plans and technical information, whereas the contradicted text refers to 'Information' in a broader sense, including verbal disclosures, electronic storage, and various forms beyond just tangible documents. This creates a direct conflict and inconsistency, making it impossible to reconcile the two clauses because the obligations in the contradicted text are now impossible to fulfill for the information that is no longer classified as Information based on the new definition.

perturbation.changed_text: 4. Each party shall protect the 'Data' received from the other party, understanding that 'Data' refers to only customer lists and pricing information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text refers to 'Information' as defined in Section 1. The modified text introduces a new term, 'Data,' and provides a very narrow definition (customer lists and pricing information). This creates an in-text contradiction because the rest of Section 4 assumes the protection obligations apply to the broad definition of 'Information,' when now, according to this modified sentence, those obligations only apply to 'Data' (customer lists and pricing).
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) shall not directly o r indirectly d isclose, verbally, in writin g or in any other form, or allow the Inform ation to be di sclosed to any other person, fi rm or com pany;
perturbation.justification: The original text broadly defines 'Information', while the changed text narrowly defines 'Data' as only customer lists and pricing information. This creates a direct contradiction because the contradicted text refers to 'Information' and prohibits its disclosure. Since the changed text limits the protection to 'Data,' it implies that other types of 'Information' are not protected, contradicting the broad prohibition in the contradicted text. Therefore, the change introduces a significant inconsistency leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. The obl igations cont ained herei n shall not appl y to Inform ation whi ch: (a) at the tim e of disclosure, is with in the public domain or which, after d isclosure, becomes rightfully available to the public, other than by a breach of this Agreem ent; (b) was independently deve loped or acquired by the receiving party, other than by a breach of this Agreem ent; (c) was rightfully obtained by the receiving party from a third party without restrictions in respect to disclosure or use; (d) the receiving party is by law com pelled to disc lose, provided that the r eceiving party has forthwith notified the disclosing party of an y such com pelled disclosure when the receiving party first becom es aware o f any such legal obligation to disclose: or (e) is explicitly ap proved for release b y written authorisatio n of the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. The obligations contained herein shall not apply to 'Intellectual Property' which: (a) at the time of disclosure, is within the public domain or which, after disclosure, becomes rightfully available to the public, other than by a breach of this Agreement;
perturbation.explanation: The original clause refers to exceptions for 'Information'. The modification replaces 'Information' with 'Intellectual Property'. Since 'Intellectual Property' isn't defined in the original text, and 'Information' has a broad definition (though modified above), this creates ambiguity. Does this exception now only apply to formal IP like patents and copyrights, or does it also include confidential business information? This change contradicts the purpose of the NDA, which is to protect all confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) was independently deve loped or acquired by the receiving party, other than by a breach of this Agreem ent;
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Information' to 'Intellectual Property' significantly narrows the scope of the exception in clause 5. The original text uses 'Information', which is broad and likely encompasses confidential business details relevant to an NDA. The contradicted text, clause (b) of the original section 5, refers to independently developed or acquired 'Information'. By changing 'Information' to 'Intellectual Property' in the exception, the modified clause creates ambiguity about whether independently developed confidential business information is still protected. This inconsistency directly contradicts the original intention of protecting a broad range of information, creating a strong in-text contradiction and directly breaks the original obligation. This also is classified as 'Misaligned Terminology' because it is using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them.

perturbation.original_text: 6. The parties’ obligations hereunder shall: (a) be unlimited in territo ry; (b) only expi re ten (10) y ears from the dat e of this Agreem ent or in the case of technical Inform ation at the end of t he com mercial useful ness t o the disclosing part y; and (c) survive the term ination of this Agreem ent for any reason whatsoever.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. The parties' obligations hereunder regarding 'Data Security' shall: (a) be limited to the United Kingdom; (b) expire five (5) years from the date of this Agreement; and (c) not survive the termination of this Agreement for any reason whatsoever.
perturbation.explanation: The original text discusses the duration and scope of obligations related to 'Information'. The modified text introduces 'Data Security,' a term not previously defined and significantly restricts the scope, duration, and survival of the obligations. This directly contradicts the general obligations outlined earlier in the agreement, creating uncertainty about which terms apply where. The original agreement does not have a definition for 'Data Security', which is already a contradiction to what is being stated. This change specifically contradicts the broad territorial scope in the original (6a) and the survival of obligations after termination (6c).
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces 'Data Security' and restricts the obligations to the United Kingdom (6a), while the contradicted text states that the obligations are 'unlimited in territory.' This is a direct conflict. The changed text also states that the obligations do not survive termination (6c) conflicting with the original text (6c).

perturbation.original_text: 7. The parties acknowledge that the obligations contai ned herein are entirely reasonable and necessary in order to protect th e leg itimate co mmercial, trad e, business an d tech nical in terests o f the disclosing party and t he expi ration of such obl igations i s without prejudi ce to any rights whi ch the disclosing part y may have i n relation to the Inform ation under appl icable Intellectual Propert y law.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. The parties acknowledge that the obligations contained herein are not reasonable or necessary for the protection of the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: This clause originally affirms the reasonableness and necessity of the NDA's obligations. The modified text directly contradicts this by stating the obligations are *not* reasonable or necessary. This creates a significant in-text contradiction, undermining the entire basis of the agreement and its enforceability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. The parties acknowledge that a vi olation by the receiving party of its obligations contained herein m ay cause serio us harm to the disclosing party th at damages may be insufficien t to constitute an adequate remedy. The parties ag ree th at they sh all be entitled , in addition to all o ther rights provided by law o r by this Agreem ent, including m onetary dam ages, t o obtain a perm anent injunct ion or m andatory court order to prevent the violation of t he obl igations cont ained herei n.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the obligations were reasonable and necessary to protect the disclosing party's interests. The changed text directly states that these obligations are *not* reasonable or necessary. This directly contradicts the contradicted text (clause 8), which assumes that violation of these obligations will cause 'serious harm' and justifies injunctive relief. If the obligations aren't reasonable or necessary, as per the changed text, it undermines the justification for the remedies in clause 8, creating a significant contradiction. The change fits the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, because it contradicts other definitions found within the document.

perturbation.original_text: 10. The part ies underst and and agree t hat no cont ract or agreem ent with regard t o the Purpose, nor any other undert aking or arrangem ent involving the part ies, is deem ed to have been m ade or ent ered into by reason of this Ag reem ent.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. The parties understand and agree that this agreement constitutes a binding contract with regard to 'Project Alpha', a specific undertaking involving only financial information.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause explicitly states that the NDA doesn't create any contract or agreement related to the 'Purpose'. The modified text contradicts this by stating that the agreement *does* constitute a binding contract, but only with regard to 'Project Alpha' and only concerning financial information. This creates confusion: Is the NDA a contract or not? Does it apply only to Project Alpha's financial information or more broadly as originally stated?
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. This agreem ent shall govern t he terms under whi ch one party discloses Inform ation to the other part y in relation to the Purpose, regardless of whether the disc losure takes place before or after the date of this agreem ent.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that the NDA does not create any contract or agreement related to the Purpose. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that the agreement *does* constitute a binding contract, but only with regard to 'Project Alpha' and only concerning financial information. This directly conflicts with clause 2, which states that the agreement governs the terms under which one party discloses Information to the other party in relation to the Purpose, regardless of timing. The change introduces a direct conflict, creating an in-text contradiction because the agreement now both is and is not a contract, and its scope is both limited and unlimited, based on which clause is being enforced. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.
